stemHtml: A flight crew erroneously calculates a V1 speed that is equal to the VMCG (118 knots) and less than the VMCA (120 knots). During take-off, they experience an engine failure just before reaching V1 but decide to continue the takeoff. What is the most likely outcome given these circumstances?

options.a: The aircraft will continue straight ahead, achieving lift-off without lateral deviation.
options.b: The aircraft will likely overshoot the runway laterally due to insufficient control below VMCA.
options.c: The aircraft will safely lift off but will experience reduced climb performance.
options.d: The aircraft will exceed its designed tire speed ratings but maintain directional control.
